phenomenon: visual cue influence on judgment and action
claim: Perceptual anchors automatically influence quantity judgment and subsequent consumption unless cognitive resources are depleted.

phenomenon: literacy intervention efficacy in semi-transparent orthography
claim: Structured, multi-component early reading intervention improves outcomes in at-risk children regardless of software adaptivity.

phenomenon: implicit classical conditioning of brain response
claim: Neural signatures of classical conditioning emerge even when the unconditioned stimulus is perceptually masked and not consciously identified.
Perceived government impartiality reduces rent-seeking and ethnic conflict, enabling sustained growth independent of policy content.

phenomenon: operational simplicity and discipline enable flexibility
claim: Manufacturing flexibility arises from disciplined simplicity in process design and execution, not from trade-offs or sequential development.
Repeated interaction with a stimulus degrades subjective clarity and confidence in memory for that stimulus via shift from perceptual to conceptual processing.
